mechanism_narrative: A persistent low and stable federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future equity cash flows, supporting a continued uptrend in forward S&P 500 returns over the next month as capital searches for yield in risk assets.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS=1.16% as of 2017-08-01, well below historical average
failure_condition: Rank IC between FEDFUNDS level and 20-day forward SPX return falls below 0.03 over any rolling 2-year window, or positive-return hit rate in low-rate periods is statistically indistinguishable from baseline (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When UNRATE rises by more than 0.3 percentage points over a rolling 3-month window, it signals an inflection toward labor market deterioration that historically precedes equity market drawdowns over the following quarter as earnings expectations are revised down.
failure_condition: Conditional on the trigger firing, 60-day forward SPX returns are positive in more than 55% of historical instances, or the mean triggered return is not significantly below the unconditional mean (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous deceleration in retail sales (RSAFS) and housing starts (HOUST) jointly indicates weakening consumer and residential investment demand, a combination that is more predictive of near-term equity softness than either series alone because it captures both discretionary spending and durable-goods-linked capex intentions.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS 3-month annualized growth < 0 AND HOUST 3-month annualized growth < -5%
failure_condition: Joint-decline signal produces 20-day forward returns statistically indistinguishable from unconditional baseline (p > 0.10), or the interaction term adds no incremental Rank IC over either single-series model.

mechanism_narrative: In a late-cycle expansion regime, continued acceleration of industrial production (INDPRO) reflects capacity utilization pressures that raise input costs and compress margins, causing the historically positive INDPRO-equity relationship to weaken or invert over a quarterly horizon.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO year-over-year growth exceeds 2.5% while economy is in late-cycle expansion
failure_condition: During late-cycle periods with high INDPRO growth, 60-day forward SPX returns remain positive and statistically indistinguishable from expansion-phase baseline (p > 0.05), rejecting the margin-compression channel.

mechanism_narrative: Steady positive month-over-month growth in nonfarm payrolls (PAYEMS) signals ongoing labor income expansion that supports near-term consumer spending and risk appetite, producing a persistent short-horizon positive drift in equity returns.
failure_condition: 5-day forward SPX return correlation with PAYEMS monthly change is not statistically different from zero (p > 0.10) over any rolling 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate rises faster than trailing CPI inflation such that the real policy rate crosses from negative to positive, it signals a genuine tightening regime that compresses valuation multiples and produces negative equity returns over the subsequent quarter, distinct from nominal rate moves alone.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS minus trailing 12-month CPIAUCSL inflation crosses from negative to positive
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS=1.16%, CPIAUCSL YoY near 2% as of 2017-08
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns following a real-rate sign flip are not significantly more negative than the unconditional baseline (p > 0.05) across at least two distinct tightening episodes.

mechanism_narrative: Joint acceleration in real GDP growth (GDPC1) alongside retail sales growth (RSAFS) reflects broad-based demand strength that supports upward earnings revisions and sustained equity appreciation over a quarterly horizon, with the combined signal outperforming either series individually because it filters out sector-specific noise.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 QoQ annualized growth > 2% AND RSAFS 3-month annualized growth > 3%
regime_hint: mid-cycle expansion with broad demand strength
failure_condition: Conditional on both thresholds being met, 60-day forward SPX returns fail to exceed unconditional mean returns by a statistically significant margin (p > 0.05) across at least three historical episodes.

mechanism_narrative: Short-horizon momentum in industrial production (INDPRO) month-over-month changes reflects real-time manufacturing activity that markets incorporate quickly, producing a modest but persistent positive drift in equity prices over the following trading week.
failure_condition: 5-day forward SPX return Rank IC with INDPRO month-over-month change remains below 0.02 over any rolling 2-year window.
